meta.reportLabel: Sun Report — dollar long validated but FADING (DXY off 2 sessions ~101.2, Sept-hike odds sub-50%) into the big week: quarter-end (Jun 30), ECB Sintra (Jul 1-2) + US jobs (Jul 2, ~110k). Brent ~$72 (4-mo low) pins CAD at a 14-mo low; oil leg still the cleaner edge
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40
meta.regime: Dollar-strong but fading at the margin, into the busiest week of the quarter. May core PCE confirmed hot (3.4% / headline 4.1%) yet landed in line, so the dollar has slipped two sessions (DXY ~101.2) and Sept-hike odds leaked below 50% (~70% for at least one hike by Sept). The crowded long now faces quarter-end rebalancing (Jun 29-30), the ECB Sintra forum (Jul 1-2) and a June jobs print (Jul 2) seen easing to ~110k from +172k. The cleaner edge stays the oil leg: Brent ~$72 (4-mo low, lowest since Feb 27, -10%+ on the week), pinning CAD at a 14-mo low (USD/CAD ~1.42). USD/JPY ~161.5, squeezed just under the 162.00 intervention line.
meta.nextBigEvent: Quarter-end rebalancing (Jun 29-30), ECB Sintra (Jul 1-2), then US June jobs / NFP (Jul 2, ~110k exp.) — the dollar's gauntlet

dailyRead: The dollar long is validated but fading — and now runs into the busiest week of the quarter. May core PCE confirmed hot (3.4%, hottest since Oct 2023; headline 4.1%), but because it landed in line, DXY slipped two sessions into the weekend to ~101.2 and Sept-hike odds sit sub-50% (~70% for at least one hike by Sept). The crowded long now faces a four-event gauntlet: quarter-end rebalancing (Jun 29-30), the ECB Sintra forum (Jul 1-2), US ISM (Jul 1) and a June jobs print (Thu Jul 2) seen easing to ~110k from May's +172k. The cleaner edge stays the oil leg: Brent closed ~$72 (4-month low, lowest since Feb 27, -10%+ on the week), pinning CAD at a 14-month low (USD/CAD ~1.42). Best-expressed trades: USD/CAD long, AUD/CAD long, EUR/JPY short; the structural long-JPY core (USD/JPY short, JPYBASKET long) stays squeezed at ~161.5, just under the 162.00 intervention line — defend, do not add.

strength.tag: hot PCE confirmed, but Sept odds sub-50%
strength.drivers: Core PCE 3.4% (May, up from 3.3%); headline PCE 4.1%; CPI 4.2%; NFP +172k (May), Jun seen ~110k. Fed 3.50-3.75%, hawkish hold, dots flip next move to a HIKE.
strength.forward: 🦅 Validated but fading: hot PCE confirms Warsh, but the in-line print + soft-NFP fears nudged Sept-hike odds sub-50%; DXY off 2 sessions to ~101.2. Risk: a soft Jul 2 jobs is the clean fade. Next FOMC late Jul.

strength.drivers: Core 2.6% (May); services CPI 3.7% (up from 3.2%); CPI 2.8% (held). BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7-2 hold (Greene + Pill hike votes); next BoE Jul 30 + MPR.
strength.forward: 🦅 Hawkish — services re-accelerated to 3.7%, reopening a hike-tail and keeping 2 dissents alive into the Jul 30 MPR. Risk: a soft activity wave re-splits it. Next BoE Jul 30.

strength.drivers: May CPI: trimmed mean 3.6% (up from 3.4%); headline ~4.0%, trim peaks ~3.9% Q2; unemp 4.5%. RBA 4.35%, unanimous Jun hold, on hold to year-end.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 Sticky 3.6% trim keeps an Aug-hike tail open but analysts doubt it forces a move; AUD/USD ~0.692, capped sub-0.70. Risk: soft Q2 jobs. Next RBA Aug 11.

strength.drivers: Headline ~2.5%, recovering; unemp ~5.1%. RBNZ 2.25% (held May 29); firm Q1 GDP. Oil's slide cut the imported-inflation case — market now leans HOLD Jul 9 (was ~85% priced for a hike); ASB pushes the first hike to Sept.
strength.forward: ⚖️ DOVISH REPRICING: oil's slide killed the cost-shock case, so the Jul 9 hike is no longer priced (hold lean; ASB sees first hike Sept, OCR ~3.0% by year-end); UBS still argues a hike on de-anchoring risk → split. Softens the NZD floor. Next RBNZ Jul 9 (hold lean).

strength.drivers: Core ~1.5%; CPI ~2.8%. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16), Summary of Opinions favours more hikes; spot ~161.5 (just under the 162.00 intervention line).
strength.forward: 🦅↔ Destination unchanged, spot squeezed. US-Japan coordinated-action readiness reaffirmed, but markets doubt Tokyo's will after April's reserve-draining op — two-sided, the swing. Next BoJ late Jul.

strength.drivers: HICP 3.2% (May, held; energy +10.9%); core 2.2% (down from 2.3%); sees HICP 3.0% '26 -> 2.0% '28; unemp ~6.3%. ECB 2.25% (hiked Jun 11), framed as one-off war insurance, not a cycle.
strength.forward: ⚖️🔻 Pure USD-leg: a hot-but-fading Fed pushes EUR/USD down, dovish-frame ECB caps bounces. Sintra forum Jul 1-2 — watch for any walk-back. Next ECB Jul 23.

strength.drivers: May CPI 3.2% (up from 2.8%, gasoline +33% y/y); trimmed core 2.0%, median 2.1%. BoC 2.25% (4th hold, Jun 10), looking through energy (oil still ~$10/bbl above April MPR).
strength.forward: ⚖️🔻 Hot/broadening CPI gives CAD a floor, but Brent's slide to ~$72 (4-mo low) pins the loonie at a 14-mo low (USD/CAD ~1.42); BoC looks through energy. Risk: oil re-spike rescues it. Next BoC late Jul.

symbols.why: Cleanest pair: validated-hot US PCE vs CAD at a 14-mo low on Brent's slide to ~$72 (-10%+/wk). Hot CPI 3.2% only a floor (core ~2%); ~1.42, near a 52-wk high.
symbols.risk: An oil re-spike (MOU stall / Lebanon) rescues CAD; quarter-end USD selling.

symbols.why: Cleanest commodity split: sticky AUD trim (3.6%) + RBA floor vs oil-pressed, look-through-CPI CAD; Brent ~$72 keeps the tilt widening.
symbols.risk: Risk-off hits AUD beta harder; a CAD-supportive oil bid.

symbols.why: Weak yen HELPS this: structural BoJ-tightening destination + a capped, dovish-frame EUR — cleaner than the USD/JPY leg now the dollar fades.
symbols.risk: A coordination-flavoured intervention spike snaps every XXX/JPY lower at once.

symbols.why: Structural-JPY destination vs a CAD pinned by sub-$72 oil; firmer Canadian CPI only a mild headwind to the short.

symbols.why: Structural core (BoJ hiking, coord watch) but SQUEEZED ~161.5, just under 162.00. Defend, do NOT add here.
symbols.risk: Clean break above 162 = momentum higher; a JOINT intervention spike is the violent re-load.

symbols.risk: The dovish RBNZ repricing undercuts NZD further; an oil bid lifts CAD.

symbols.why: USD headwind keeps it sub-0.70, but conviction trimmed as the dollar fades + 3.6% trim arms a floor — two-sided into Jul 2.
symbols.risk: A soft Jul 2 NFP + sticky core flips the leg — the firm-AUD invalidation.

symbols.why: Two firm legs: services CPI 3.7% re-armed a hawkish BoE vs a fading-but-hot dollar — no clean edge.
symbols.risk: Soft Jul 2 jobs lifts it; soft UK activity re-splits it.

symbols.why: Shift vs Sat: the priced Jul RBNZ hike that floored NZD is gone (now a hold lean), so the soft-NZD leg re-opens vs a still-firm dollar — leans modestly short.
symbols.risk: A soft Jul 2 US jobs or a hot NZ Q2 CPI flips it; risk-on lifts NZD.

symbols.why: Services-CPI re-acceleration firmed the GBP leg, offsetting the yen destination — two-sided.
symbols.risk: Firm GBP + weak yen spot tilts it up; a coordinated intervention spike snaps it lower.

symbols.why: Weak yen leans it down, but risk-off carry unwind + RBA floor make it modest only.
symbols.risk: Risk-on carry holds it up; a coordinated spike is the abrupt re-load.

symbols.why: Structural JPY destination vs a now-softer NZD (RBNZ hold repricing) — carry offsets near-term, low conviction.
symbols.risk: Risk-on carry dominates near-term -> stalls; an intervention spike re-loads.

symbols.why: ECB done-dovish vs a hawkish-hold RBA (sticky trim mean) — mild AUD tilt, no USD vector.
symbols.risk: EZ vs Aussie data set direction.

symbols.why: ECB spent vs a now-softer NZD (Jul hold repricing) — the slight NZD tilt has faded to neutral.
symbols.risk: NZD risk beta or a Jul 23 ECB shift.

symbols.why: Two firm legs: re-armed BoE (services 3.7%) vs hawkish-hold RBA (3.6% trim) — no clean edge.
symbols.risk: UK services vs Aussie sticky-core split sets direction.

symbols.why: Hawkish-re-armed GBP vs a softening NZD (RBNZ hold) — mild GBP tilt, but low conviction.
symbols.risk: Trade levels only; a hot NZ Q2 CPI closes the gap.

symbols.why: Both commodity-bloc; the RBNZ-hold repricing nudges a slight AUD tilt (RBA keeps the live hike-tail) but stays a wash.
symbols.risk: Aussie data vs RBNZ Jul 9.

symbols.why: Downgraded from MEDIUM: PCE validated the leg but the index faded 2 sessions to ~101.2 and Sept odds slipped sub-50%; EUR/JPY/GBP/CAD still soft but the edge bleeds into a heavy week.
symbols.risk: Quarter-end (Jun 30) rebalancing + a soft Jul 2 NFP fade a stretched long; positioning extreme.

symbols.why: Squeezed leg: basket near lows as the yen sits ~161.5, just under the 162 line — core only, do NOT add. (Rises when JPY strengthens.)
symbols.risk: Wait for a coordinated intervention or a USD pullback to re-load; thesis intact, timing wrong.

symbols.why: Oil tailwind stronger (Brent 4-mo low ~$72) + EZ PMI downturn easing; fading-Fed less of a cap, but cancelled talks/Lebanon widen the tail.

catalysts.note: Q2 close; rebalancing flows can squeeze the crowded dollar long regardless of macro — the nearest tactical risk.
Lagarde & co. speak; watch for any walk-back of the 'one-off, no cycle' framing — the EUR swing.
An early read on whether goods inflation is rolling over with oil.

catalysts.event: US June jobs report (NFP) — early for Jul 4
catalysts.note: Seen easing to ~110k from May's +172k. A soft print is the cleanest fade of the validated-but-fading dollar long.
HOLD now leaned (was a hike) after the oil drop; the dovish repricing is the NZD swing.
Services 3.7% keeps 2 hawk dissents live — the GBP swing.
USD/JPY ~161.5 vs the 162.00 line; coordinated-action readiness reaffirmed but doubted — biggest near-term JPY-book swing.
Brent ~$72 (4-mo low) on Hormuz flows ~75% of prewar; postponed talks + a drone incident keep a respike tail.

geopolitics: Flows continue to crush the war premium. With Strait-of-Hormuz transits back to ~75% of pre-war volumes, Brent closed near $72 on Friday — a fresh 4-month low (lowest since Feb 27), down over 10% on the week and ~40%+ off the wartime peak. The diplomatic track is messier than the tape: the Geneva technical round was abruptly postponed and there were fresh ceasefire-violation accusations after a drone struck a tanker near Oman — but the strait stayed open and traffic held, so the base case (truce holds, oil drifts lower) is intact with a live respike tail. Transmission: oil → energy/headline inflation → CB hawkishness → FX. Base case keeps a (now-fading) dollar bid, supports GER40 and risk-beta AUD/NZD on calm days, and does NOT buy a Fed cut (hot May PCE confirmed inflation marked up into the oil drop; disinflation is energy-led, not broad); for CAD the hot CPI (3.2%, gasoline +33% y/y) only partly offsets the deeper oil drag → soft-and-capped, the export side pressed hardest at ~$72 (loonie at a 14-mo low), though BoC notes oil is still ~$10/bbl above its April MPR assumption. Tail (alive): an MOU stall / Lebanon escalation re-spikes oil (rescues CAD, hurts AUD/NZD/GER40, hands USD & JPY competing haven bids) — the persistent ~$4,200 gold bid is the thin premium for exactly this.
